IRFC SIGNS ₹13,527 CR HYDERABAD METRO REFINANCING DEAL 🚆💰

• 🤝 IRFC signed ₹13,527 Cr refinancing agreement with L&T Metro Rail (Hyderabad)

• 🚇 Deal aimed at refinancing Hyderabad Metro Rail debt obligations

• 🏙️ Marks one of India’s landmark urban rail financing transactions

• 🇮🇳 Supports Government’s urban mobility & Viksit Bharat vision

• 📍 Hyderabad Metro Phase-1 spans 69.2 km across 3 corridors with 57 stations

• 👥 Metro network currently serves over 5 lakh daily passengers

• 💵 Refinancing to replace higher-cost debt with competitively priced long-term funding

• 📆 Structured over 20-year tenure with quarterly repayments

• 🛡️ Backed by Telangana Government undertaking, guarantee & RBI-backed debit mandate

• 🚄 IRFC sees this as a major step in diversifying beyond traditional railway financing

🟢 Impact: Positive — Large refinancing deal strengthens IRFC’s infrastructure financing portfolio and boosts visibility in urban mobility funding opportunities.

text:
JPM on LTM
Downgrade to N, TP cut to Rs 4500 from Rs 5100

LTM announced that it has issued an offer to acquire Randstad’s technology and consulting services business in Europe and Australia.

Revenues of these entities (€469m) have declined sharply in the past two years while estimate business operates at much lower 4-5% Ebitda margins, given its onshore-heavy nature, which should be materially margin-dilutive.

LTM has also announced concomitant contracts to take over and build a GCC for Randstad in India and a reverse contract to Randstad to support LTM’s subcon needs
While, across three contracts, management doesn’t see any EPS dilution, estimate acquisition alone could drive 2% EPS dilution due to amortization costs and lower interest income.

Moreover, don’t see obvious horizontal service line or vertical synergies, given its focus on digital engineering and cybersecurity rather than core IT Services, which LTM focuses on.
Cross-sell opportunities as they exist should be limited by different buying centers for engineering and IT services

Acquisition price appears to be modest at €160m on a EV/Sales basis (0.34x), but not on implied profit multiples (8x EBITDA, 13-15x EBIT), given global/EU peer multiples.

More than price paid, see this acquisition as a sign that organic growth opportunities are drying up and management is being forced to evaluate unusual acquisitions to bulk up rather than thoughtfully add capabilities. Fear management distraction, due to closure of the acquisition by 3Q27 and ekeing out of synergies after that, could weigh on execution.

Moderate organic revenues/EPS by 1-2%, cut target PE multiple from 21x to 19x

Power Mech mgmt : Q4 FY26 concall

India may enter a massive thermal power capex cycle

Atlanta Electricals Q4FY26 Concall Highlights

✔️ Demand 

• Transformer demand to exceed supply for next 5 years 🔥
• 1GW power addition needs ~8–11x transformer capacity addition 
• BESS/grid stability to further increase transformer demand

🗣 “For every 1GW capacity addition, transformer capacity addition is ~8–11x across KV classes.”

✔️ Order Book & Execution 

• ~80–85% of order book executable within 12 months 🔥
• 220KV orders have ~12–18 month execution cycle
• Export order inflows expected in FY27

🗣 “Approximately 75% of the order book is with price variation clause.”

✔️ Margins 

• ~75% order book has price variation clause
• 400KV & 765KV products can add ~200bps higher margins 🔥
• Margin guidance remains stable

🗣 “400KV & 765KV class products could have ~200bps higher margins.”

✔️ Capacity Expansion 

• Vadod utilization: ~39% (FY26) → ~65% (FY27) → 100% next FY
• FY27 mix largely 220KV with gradual 400KV ramp-up
• Focus remains on 400KV & 765KV development

🗣 “Vadod facility utilization is expected to reach ~65% this year and 100% next year.”

✔️ Outlook 

• Management reiterated ~40% CAGR growth aspiration for next 3 years 🔥
• HVDC seen as future opportunity, but near-term focus remains on 400KV/765KV execution

🗣 “We maintain a standard of growing at 40% CAGR for next 3 years.”

• 🤝 Company actively engaging with central & state authorities for faster payment clearances

• 📅 Management expects meaningful recovery of outstanding dues by end of CY2026

• ⚙️ Focus remains on execution ramp-up, disciplined cost management & improving cash cycles

text:
Captain Polyplast Reports Strong Q4 FY26 Performance with 80% Growth in Total Income & 91% Surge in Net Profit; FY26 Income Rises 45%

Captain Polyplast Limited, one of the leading manufacturer and exporter of micro irrigation solutions, and has diversified its operations into the fast-paced solar EPC and polymer markets, has reported its Audited financials for Q4 & 12M FY26.

Consolidated Key Financial Highlights Q4 FY26

- Total Income of ₹ 142.22 Cr, YoY growth of 80%
- EBITDA of ₹ 14.16 Cr, YoY growth of 66%
- EBITDA Margin of 9.96%
- Net profit of ₹ 9.76 Cr, YoY growth of 91%
- Net Profit Margin of 6.87%, YoY growth of 40 Bps
- EPS of ₹ 1.64, YoY growth of 82%

Consolidated Key Financial Highlights FY26

- Total Income of ₹ 419.75 Cr, YoY growth of 45%
- EBITDA of ₹ 46.32 Cr, YoY growth of 36%
- EBITDA Margin of 11.03%
- Net Profit of ₹ 27.78 Cr
- Net Profit Margin of 6.62%
- EPS of ₹ 4.65

Note - The Net Profit in FY25 includes as exceptional gain of ₹15.61 Cr hence the YoY figures of Net Profit, Net Profit Margin & EPS are not comparable.

Operational Highlight

- The Company received an order from MSEDCL under the “Magel Tyala Saur Krishi Pump” Yojna for supplying and installing 300 off-grid solar water pumps, with a total order value of ₹8.17 Cr.
- The company has also started operations at its Ahmedabad facility which is spread across 70,000 Sq Ft. on 14th May, 2026. The Facility is designed to enable in-house production of critical components, supporting backward integration

Currently, the stock is trading at ₹ 80.09, with a Mcap of ₹ 481.97 Cr. (As on 22nd May 2026)

text:
GLENMARK : GLENMARK USA TO PAY $29.6 MN SETTLEMENT IN U.S. ANTITRUST CASE ⚖️💊

• 🇺🇸 Glenmark USA entered settlement agreement with U.S. state attorneys general in generic drug pricing antitrust litigation

• 💰 Settlement amount stands at $29.628 Million (~₹250 Cr), payable over 5 years

• ⚖️ Cases relate to allegations of price-fixing, market allocation & anti-competitive conduct in generic drugs market

• 🏛️ Glenmark USA had been named in over 35 complaints since 2016 along with other generic drug manufacturers

• 🚫 Company denied all allegations and stated settlement does not constitute admission of wrongdoing or liability

• 📊 Full settlement amount has already been provided for in company financials

• 📌 Company stated settlement will not have a significant impact on its financial position

🟡 Impact: Neutral (Settlement resolves long-pending litigation uncertainty, while financial impact remains manageable as provisions were already made)

BIGGEST COMEBACK STOCKS OF THE YEAR 🚀📈  

Top Performers
- Sterlite Technologies: +522%
- MTAR Technologies: +472%
- Bajaj Consumer Care: +225%
- Quality Power Electrical: +212%
- Venus Remedies: +211%

Strong Gainers
- Ather Energy: +195%
- GE Power: +193%
- TD Power: +187%
- Acutaas Chemicals: +183%
- GE Vernova T&D: +173%

Other Major Movers

- Lumax Auto Technologies: +160%
- Hindustan Copper: +148%
- Sansera Engineering: +139%
- HFCL: +137%

Key Trend
- Rally led by power, defence, EMS, railways, capital goods and manufacturing themes
- Several stocks more than doubled from yearly lows

BANK OF BARODA – FY27 FISCAL DEFICIT WARNING  

Key Estimates
- West Asia conflict could widen FY27 fiscal deficit to 4.7%-4.8% of GDP
- Estimated fiscal gap may rise to ~₹2.1 lakh Cr

Key Risks
- Higher fertiliser subsidies and possible fuel excise cuts
- OMCs facing estimated daily losses of ~₹750 Cr

Offsetting Factor
- RBI’s record ₹2.86 lakh Cr dividend may partly cushion fiscal pressure

SEBI CRACKS DOWN ON FINFLUENCER-LED STOCK MANIPULATION

Hemant Gupta and 6 others accused in SEBI's ₹20 crore SME stock pump-and-dump investigation

SEBI alleges family-run network bought shares and then used X, Telegram, WhatsApp to promote them publicly

SEBI alleges investors lured using “multibagger”, “upper circuit” style stock messaging

Probe claims accused sold shares after retail investors pushed prices higher

RPP INFRA – NEW WORK ORDER  

Order Details
- Received ₹5.13 Cr SIPCOT work order including GST
- Project located at SIPCOT Industrial Park, Shoolagiri
- Scope includes removal of retrieved hard rock materials

Execution Update
- Project execution timeline of 12 months
- Work to commence within 15 days of order receipt
- Company to deposit ₹25.65 Lakhs as security amount

UKMTO: 

Incident involving an oil tanker near Yemen:

- The UK Maritime Operations Organization today reported receiving information about a security incident involving an oil tanker in waters near the island of Socotra.

IGL hikes CNG prices again 🚨

⏩₹1/kg increase from 6 am, May 23-third hike this month, total up ₹4/kg

⏩Move driven by higher input costs & strong USD, but CNG still up to 45% cheaper vs alternate fuels

Nikkei briefly dipped under 60k as AI and semiconductor stocks crashed. JPM says it’s a technical overheating issue, not a fundamental economic crisis. A short-term cooling off.

JPM’s Z-score model shows momentum surged to +3.90, then crashed to -5.30. Expect momentum to stay dead for 1 month. Smart move? Rotate money into classic Value stocks.

Regional banks are the ultimate hedge right now. They mix cheap Value with steady Momentum. Plus, they win big as the BOJ stays hawkish and hikes interest rates from June.

🎯 JPM Summary: Don't panic dump. Tech is just cooling down. Lock your capital into Japanese regional banks. They offer zero pain trades and clear profits from rising interest rates.

CNG has become more expensive in New Delhi following a Rs 1 price hike reportedly issued by Indraprastha Gas Ltd. 

This marks the third time CNG prices have been raised in the last 10 days. 

The government had raised CNG prices by Rs 2 on May 15 and then again by Re 1 on May 18.

GLENMARK PHARMACEUTICALS – U.S. LITIGATION SETTLEMENT  

Settlement Details
- Glenmark USA to pay $29.63 Million over 5 years
- Settlement related to U.S. antitrust litigation
- Agreement subject to court approval

Company Statement
- Company denied allegations and liability
- Financial impact expected to be limited due to existing provisions

WINDSOR MACHINES – LAND ACQUISITION UPDATE  

Acquisition Details
- Acquired ~77,198 sq. meters land in Rajkot, Gujarat
- Transaction value stands at ₹55 Cr
- Ownership transfer completed on May 22, 2026

Business Impact
- Land acquired for future industrial expansion
- Supports long-term business growth plans

Drone gets DGCA Certification allowing commercial deployment across India for survey, mapping, aerial surveillance and infra inspection.

More Civil use case for Ideaforge to diversify from defence.

- Change due to equity dilution and not stake sale by Linde India

I Will Lead A Reform-Oriented Fed

With Independence And Resolve, Inflation Can Be Lower

Coming Years Can Bring Unmatched Prosperity

*   **Afcom Holdings Limited**
*   **K2 Infragen Limited**
*   **Sona Machinery Limited**

Other scrips frequently referenced as examples of their social media manipulation, X (Twitter) posts, and private WhatsApp/Telegram chat instructions include **Kalyani Cast-Tech Ltd**, **Kay Cee Energy & Infra Ltd**, **Influx Healthtech Ltd**, and **Freshara Agro Exports Ltd**. FORTIS HEALTHCARE – Q4 HIGHLIGHTS  

Hospital Business
- Hospital revenue rose 19% YoY to ₹2,023 Cr
- EBITDA increased 19.9% YoY to ₹446 Cr
- Occupied beds grew 17% YoY

Operational Highlights
- ARPOB improved to ₹2.56 Cr p.a.
- Radiation Therapy & Robotic Surgery volumes grew strongly
- International patient revenue increased 18.5% YoY in FY26

Expansion Update
- Added ~500 beds through acquisitions & leases
- Acquired People Tree Hospital and Shrimann Hospital
- Diagnostics network expanded to 4,445 touch points

Diagnostics & Preventive Care
- Diagnostics revenue grew 11.1% YoY to ₹387 Cr
- Diagnostics EBITDA rose 35.9% YoY to ₹85 Cr
- Preventive healthcare revenue grew 21% YoY in FY26

DAM CAPITAL – MANAGEMENT COMMENTARY  

Business Update
- Merchant Banking revenue impacted by delayed IPO launches
- Geopolitical uncertainties affected deal timelines
- Broking revenue moderated amid volatile markets

Operational Update
- Broking business continued to provide stability
- Employee count increased to 130 from 125 YoY
- Rental expenses increased due to new office premise

IRFC – ECB FUNDRAISING UPDATE  

Fundraising Details
- Raised USD 1.1 Billion ECB loan in JPY
- Consortium includes SBI, HDFC Bank, SMBC & DBS Bank
- Loan tenure stands at 5 years

Utilisation & Structure
- Funds to support railway and infrastructure projects
- Benchmark linked to Overnight TONAR
- Facility raised on unsecured basis

Strategic Update
- First ECB fundraising by IRFC in FY27
- Aims to optimize borrowing costs and support expansion

PINE LABS – BLOCK DEAL UPDATE  

Transaction Details
- Madison India Opportunities sold 2.5 Cr shares
- Deal executed at ₹144 per share

Key Buyers
- ICICI Prudential MF bought 20.8 lakh shares
- HSBC MF bought 61.7 lakh shares
- Transactions indicate institutional participation

GUJARAT THEMIS BIOSYN – OVERSEAS ACQUISITION  

Acquisition Details
- To acquire Japan-based MicroBiopharm Japan for ~₹1,300 Cr
- Deal to be executed via Themis Biosyn Japan Ltd
- Acquisition funded through debt and equity

Target Business
- MBJ operates in APIs, intermediates & CDMO services
- Strong capabilities in biotech and microbial fermentation
- Portfolio includes oncology APIs, plasmid DNA & ADC conjugation

Strategic Impact
- Strengthens global biotech manufacturing presence
- Expands fermentation-based pharma capabilities

W.H. BRADY – PROPERTY STATUS UPDATE  

Property Update
- Converted Brady House property from leasehold to freehold
- Property located at Fort, Mumbai

Transaction Details
- Paid ₹8.98 Cr as conversion charges
- Land area stands at 1,012.54 sq. meters

Regulatory Update
- Property status changed to Occupier Class I
- Updated property documents received on May 21, 2026

VIKRAN ENGINEERING Q4 

APPROVED UPTO RS 400 CR FUND RAISING VIA EQUITY

GANESH INFRAWORLD – CONTRACT TERMINATION  

Project Update
- Terminated ₹76.11 Cr Shirdi project contract in Maharashtra
- Project involved construction of 14 residential towers

Scope Details
- Included civil, structural, finishing & infrastructure works
- Project comprised 408 flats and 32 shops

Business Impact
- Order book to reduce by ₹76.11 Cr
- Original completion timeline was July 31, 2025

RAMCO CEMENTS – Q4 UPDATE  

Capacity Expansion
- Targets ~31 MTPA cement capacity by FY27
- Expansion through debottlenecking & brownfield projects

Operational Update
- 15 MW WHRS and Kiln Line-2 commissioning expected in FY27
- Projects linked to Kolimigundala plant expansion

Asset Monetisation
- Monetized ₹1,098 Cr via non-core asset sales in last 2 years
- Plans to sell additional ~₹150 Cr non-core assets

TVS SUPPLY CHAIN – ACQUISITION UPDATE  

Acquisition Details
- FIT 3PL to acquire 100% stake in Swamy & Sons 3PL
- Invested ₹59.56 Cr for initial 80% stake
- Remaining stake to be acquired by Sept 2027

Strategic Impact
- Strengthens FMCG & FMCD logistics capabilities
- Expected to support revenue and profit growth

Target Company Turnover
- FY23: ₹215.4 Cr
- FY24: ₹210.3 Cr
- FY25: ₹207.1 Cr

TVS MOTOR – STAKE ACQUISITION UPDATE  

Acquisition Details
- Acquired 4.90% stake in Jana Small Finance Bank
- Transaction completed through secondary stake purchase

Strategic Update
- Marks entry into banking & financial services ecosystem
- Potential synergies in vehicle financing and retail lending
- Strengthens mobility-linked financial services presence

DHRUV CONSULTANCY – NHAI ORDER WIN  

Order Details
- Received NHAI consultancy contract in Maharashtra
- Project value stands at ₹5.58 Cr excluding taxes
- Contract awarded under PBMC mode

Project Scope
- Covers NH-52 Aurangabad–Karodi–Telwadi stretch
- Includes supervision for maintenance & renewal work
- Execution timeline of 60 months

NTPC GREEN

BOARD APPROVED UPTO RS 5000 CR FUND RAISING VIA DEBT

OLECTRA GREENTECH – CREDIT RATING UPGRADE  

Rating Update
- India Ratings upgraded bank facilities rating to IND A+/Stable
- Upgrade covers ₹15,320 million bank loan facilities

Key Reasons
- Improved business risk profile
- Healthy order book position
- Strong revenue growth and stable EBITDA margins in 9MFY26

HINDUSTAN ORGANIC CHEMICALS – PLANT SHUTDOWN UPDATE  

Shutdown Details
- Kochi Phenol Plant to remain shut for 10 days from May 23, 2026
- Shutdown linked to BPCL LPG pipeline statutory testing

Operational Update
- Company to undertake maintenance activities during shutdown
- Hydrogen Peroxide Plant operations to continue normally

Business Impact
- Temporary impact on near-term phenol production
- Maintenance may improve operational reliability

- First successful discovery in shallower Sanu Formation
- Estimated Gas In Place of ~75 MMSCM

- Supports future appraisal and production expansion

SEBI took action on stock price manipulation through social media on Hemant Gupta, Rohan Gupta through their famous handles WealthSolitaire, desiwallstreet

text:
GUJARAT THEMIS BIOSYN TO ACQUIRE JAPAN-BASED MICROBIOPHARM FOR ₹1,300 CR 🇯🇵🧪

• 🤝 GTBL to acquire 100% stake in Japan-based MicroBiopharm Japan Co. Ltd. (MBJ) through wholly owned subsidiary Themis Biosyn Japan

• 💰 Deal size stands at JPY 21.5 Billion (~₹1,300 Cr), funded through mix of debt & equity

• 🧬 MBJ specializes in microbial fermentation, APIs, plasmid DNA manufacturing, ADC conjugation & enzyme-based revenue APIs

• 🌍 Acquisition strengthens GTBL’s transformation into technology-driven global CDMO platform

• 🏭 MBJ has strong expertise in precision fermentation, biotechnology & next-gen drug manufacturing

• 📊 MBJ reported FY26 revenue of JPY 9.5 Billion (~₹570 Cr)

• 📈 MBJ revenues stood at ~₹470 Cr in FY23, ~₹580 Cr in FY24 & ~₹560 Cr in FY25

• 🧪 Portfolio spans oncology APIs, plasmid DNA manufacturing & fermentation-based products

• ⏳ Transaction expected to close in Q2 FY27, subject to regulatory approvals in Japan

🟢 Impact: Positive (Large strategic acquisition strengthens GTBL’s global CDMO capabilities, technology platform and presence in high-value biologics & fermentation-based pharma manufacturing)
channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

Hohn’s High-Conviction Alpha Trap

Sir Chris Hohn runs a hyper-concentrated $21B book with roughly $14B parked in a single bet on GE Aerospace, weaponizing a multi-decade moat where zero new engine competitors have entered in 50 years.

Retail loves the "charitable billionaire" narrative, but Hohn’s extreme concentration introduces massive systemic tail risk. A single operational drawdown or aerospace de-rating would completely vaporize the fund's capital.

This regime shift highlights a structural corporate margin play: dominant global duopolies can easily pass through inflation-fueled input costs, squeezing labor stats while capturing immense, unchecked pricing power.

Bottom Line: Forget diversification. True alpha in this era is about finding structural monopolies with bulletproof unit economics that can survive any macro liquidity trap.

https://x.com/BeatTheStreet10/status/2057691741424357440?s=20
India Pharma Supercycle 2026

MORGAN STANLEY ON MARUTI SUZUKI

Maintain Overweight, Target price: ₹17,895

Maruti announces vehicle price hikes of up to ₹30,000 from June 2026

Brokerage estimates effective price hike at around 1%

Commodity cost tracker suggests 250-300bps cost headwind in Q1FY27

* Brokerage expects margins to trough in Q1FY27 due to commodity inflation and brownfield expansion costs

Peers M&M, Hyundai and Tata have already raised prices this year

Interesting Chart - Capex Required to Integrate in Solar Chain goes up significanlty

Waaree’s biggest advantage in this solar cycle may not just be demand.

It is the CAPEX barrier created by deeper backward integration.

The numbers explain it clearly:

Module-only capacity needs ~₹100–150 Cr/GW.
Cell + module integration needs ~₹500–600 Cr/GW.
Ingot + wafer + cell + module needs ~₹1,100–1,200 Cr/GW.
Full poly-to-module integration needs ~₹1,500–1,600 Cr/GW.

That is nearly a 10–17x jump in capital intensity as the value chain goes deeper.
This changes the competitive landscape.
On the supply side, fewer players can afford this scale, capital, technology, and execution capability.
On the demand side, ALMM and domestic sourcing requirements can further shift opportunities toward integrated domestic players.

So Waaree gets a dual-cycle benefit:

Competition reduces as integration becomes capital-heavy

Demand expands for approved, integrated domestic manufacturers

This is why Waaree is not just participating in the solar upcycle.

It is structurally positioned to remain one of the front-runners.

So what everybody sees as an oversupply or competitive issue may not be true

GOLDMAN SACHS ON APOLLO HOSPITALS

Maintain Buy

**Nomura on Sansera Engineering**  

**Target Price: ₹3,114 (from ₹2,342)**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

• Diversification strategy gaining strong momentum  
• Q4FY26 performance beat estimates  
• Non-auto business traction remains strong  
• ADS division driving global growth  
• FY27 revenue growth seen ~22%  
• FY28 revenue growth seen ~19%  
• FY27 EBITDA margin seen ~19%  
• FY28 EBITDA margin seen ~19.5%  
• FY27 EPS estimates raised ~8%  
• FY28 EPS estimates raised ~11%  
• Valuation rerating potential remains strong  
• Non-auto diversification improving visibility

Kalyan Capitals Ltd (KCL) – A Quiet Turnaround Story? 

What does the company do?

KCL is registered as a Non-Systemically Important Non-Deposit Taking Base Layer NBFC-ICC.

The company primarily provides funded facilities to individuals and corporates. Now, it has also entered the gold loan business — a segment seeing strong traction across India due to rising gold prices and demand for secured retail lending.

Business Momentum Improving
FY25 loan disbursement stood at ₹203.2 Cr, up ~24% YoY
Net NPA ratio remained around 3%

Valuation Looks Interesting Despite improvement in business activity:

Market Cap: ₹48.4 Cr
Enterprise Value: ₹304 Cr
P/B Ratio: 0.92x
Stock P/E: 12.6x
EV/EBITDA: 10.2x
Current Price: ₹9.21
Book Value: ₹10

The stock currently trades below book value and at a valuation lower than many listed NBFC peers.

Growth Metrics
5-Year Revenue CAGR: 39%
3-Year Revenue CAGR: 40%
TTM Growth: 25%

That kind of growth with improving operations often becomes the start of a turnaround rerating story.

EV compressed 3% over Sep’25 (+2% over Mar’25), largely led by elevated negative investment variance (almost similar impact between debt and equity for FY2026).

Maintain Overweight, Target price: ₹286

*CLSA on Nykaa*
O-P TP Rs 338
reported 28% revenue growth in 4QFY26 and its highest-ever Ebitda margin of 8.4%, up 195bps YoY, with Ebitda 8% above consensus. Beauty NSV grew 29% YoY with margin-accretive own brands up 54%, driving a +70bps Ebitda margin. Fashion saw 42% YoY NSV growth with Ebitda breakeven. Ebit missed our estimate due to higher-than-expected depreciation. We lower our FY27-28CL earnings estimates by 1%-2% on account of higher depreciation, but raise our target price from Rs328 to Rs338 to reflect faster sales growth. We expect Ebitda margin expansion to continue, driven by rising salience of own brands and operating leverage.

*Jefferies on Nykaa*
Buy TP raised to Rs 350

Medium-term outlook is quite positive, although mgmt. sounded a note of caution in context of a tough macro.

Hold, TP Rs 273

Overall, Revenue/EBITDA/EBIT growth at 28%/67%/129% YoY.
BPC reported in-line gross margins and contribution profits (+29% YoY on +27% revenue growth).
Nykaa has delivered a notable uptick in margin expansion in FY26 (6% to 7.5%) – driven by lower losses in fashion, uptick in BPC gross margins and operating leverage

Fisrst, Mamaearth grew in the teens YoY and management expects double-digit growth to continue.

FMCG EBITDA margin improves 200 bps YoY to 11%

*CLSA on ITC*
O-P, TP Rs 394
Cigarettes Ebit grew 7.2% YoY in 4QFY26 and was 15% ahead of consensus. The quarter was marked by a significant increase in cigarette taxes, which likely led to channel filling and potential product mix changes due to pricing differentials, thereby impacting overall performance during the period. Cigarettes net revenue for the year grew 8.2% YoY. FMCG revenue other than cigarettes grew 15% YoY and was 2%/5% above our estimate/consensus.

*CITI on ITC*
Sell, TP Rs 290
4Q performance is not directly comparable with prior periods given mid-quarter changes in cigarette taxation
Estimate ~7% volume decline, driving the 24% drop in cigarette net revenues, reflecting: 1) strong January demand; 2) trade inventory stocking ahead of price hikes; and 3) demand slowdown thereafter following staggered price increases to offset higher taxes.
Reported cigarette EBIT growth of ~7% is also not comparable, as it appears supported by one-off inventory gains on pre-tax revision stock
Importantly, key structural uncertainties persist, particularly around price elasticity, demand sensitivity to successive price hikes, and potential downtrading to illicit products

Hold, TP Rs 350

Impact of sharp tax hike was only partial, but 4Q also likely benefited from pipeline inventory around the cut-off date (JEFe).

Buy,  TP Rs 1475

DAM Cap on PI Ind
Sell. TP Rs 2556

FY26 performance underscores structural challenges in Agchem CSM

Legacy products (65–70% of exports) slowed sharply, with recovery back-ended to FY27, while new products remain steady but not sizeable enough to offset pressure on legacy products.

 Domestic agchem pressured by elevated channel inventories, pricing constraints, lower acreages, adverse weather, and regulatory transitions in Biologicals.

PI’s solid balance sheet and execution track record underpin its competitiveness, but growth remains uncertain due to delayed CSM exports recovery, Biologicals revival, and margin pressures from input costs and R&D/NCE launch spend.

EBITDA expansion is constrained despite stable gross margins.
Cut FY27E/FY28E EPS by 15%/16% to factor in higher opex, muted GM improvement, input cost pressures, and back-ended legacy recovery

text:
CITI on PI Ind
Sell, TP Rs 2600

At Rs3.37bn (below), PI’s 4Q EBITDA fell 26% yoy on lower CSM exports and domestic agchem revenues and lower EBITDA margin.

Pharma CDMO revenues grew 23% yoy (accounted for 7% of revenues in 4QFY26), although the segment continued to report EBITDA loss.

EBITDA was up 11% qoq, largely on seasonality.

EBITDA margin was 21.5% vs 22.0% in 3Q and 25.5% in 4QFY25.

Looking ahead while mgmt. expects revenue growth in FY27 – this is likely to be backended given relatively higher base in 1H.

With generic competition for PI’s largest molecule (pyroxasulfone) expected in US in CY26, muted outlook for the global agchem market & no immediate ramp-up of new segments
CITI Says Believe Al Theme Is In A Boom Rather Than A Bubble | We Are Underweight On Emerging Markets, But Overweight On Taiwan & South Korea

RATNAVEER PRECISION (+VE)

Maintain Buy, TP Rs 235-ICICI Direct (27% Upside Potential)

Bullishness From CCL Expansion

Jefferies on PI Ind
Buy, TP Rs 3575

Reported a miss on EBITDA and PAT.

New product growth has slowed, and pyroxasulfone is in sharp contraction.

Working capital rose sharply.
A favorable base and growth in new molecules should restore growth in FY27, with management expecting high-single-digit revenue growth

Forecast adjusted PAT Cagr of 12% over FY26-28E.

Jefferies on Jubilant Foodworks
Buy, TP cut to Rs 600 from Rs 850

With consumer tech platforms becoming preferred ways for investors to play consumption theme, JUBI's 4Q provided no reason to revisit name

Flat SSS & cautious ST margin commentary suggest wait for a turnaround continues.

Overall, 4Q beat modest JEFe.
Still, exit trajectory results in a 10-12% cut in EBITDA as mgmt aims for a calibrated price hike, which view as a prudent strategy

HSBC on Jubilant Foodworks
Downgrade to hold, TP cut to Rs 530 from Rs 530.

Like-for-like (LFL) of +0.2% had no one-off (LPG impact of 0.3-0.4%) and marked sharp moderation

1Q LFL looking marginally better but points to activation-led demand, making it a choice of growth or margins

Near-term inflation also a headwind; cut estimates

HSBC On PI Ind
Hold TP Rs 3000

4QFY26 earnings another miss given disappointment in Ag business; higher overheads pressure EBITDA margins

Zone of uncertainty: core businesses remain muted, and new businesses take time to scale up necessary investments
Cut earnings growth estimates

CLSA on Samvardhana Mortherson
O-P, TP Rs 173

4Q Ebitda margin came in at 11% (up 136bps QoQ) vs estimate of 9.7%.

Revenue grew 17% YoY, supported by strong growth in emerging businesses (aerospace +40% and consumer electronics 7.5x)

Believe a gradual ramp-up in the non-auto businesses along with rising focus in ASEAN markets will drive a 13% Cagr for organic growth over FY26-28CL.

Over & above this, there is scope for inorganic growth, considering a leverage ratio (net debt/Ebitda) at 0.5x (FY28CL) vs SAMIL being comfortable until 2.5x levels

Jefferies on Samvardhana Motherson
Buy, TP Rs 160

Expect 26% EPS CAGR over FY26-28.

Q4EBITDA grew 43% YoY, 19% above JEFe

While global auto volumes remain range-bound, SAMIL's recent acquisitions should boost growth in FY27-28; also like its expansion in electronics & aerospace

Higher commodity costs could pose some margin pressure in 1HFY27, but should get passed on to customers subsequently

Stock is at 26x FY27E PE vs last 5-year average of 22x.

CITI on Ola
Sell, TP Rs 26

4Q lower than estimates due to lower ASPs (partly due to accounting changes for extended warranty).

Gross margins are impressive, even adjusted for PLI.

Mgmt. is positive on EV demand, particularly in backdrop of rising fuel prices.

Ola’s market share is expected to improve as co focuses on enhancing its service offering.

1QFY27 guidance: 40-45k orders; Rs5-5.5bn revenue.

Mgmt. emphasized its efforts on cost reduction and cash flow generation (positive FCF for auto-business in 4Q).

While acknowledge Ola’s gross margin improvement, we’d look for sustained volume growth before getting more constructive
90% of Ola’s opex is fixed, & if volumes do not improve, EBITDA would be under pressure despite high gross margins

HSBC on OLA
Reduce, TP cut to Rs 33

Ola is focusing on sustainable volume growth & margin expansion after a year-long efficiency improvement programme

Cautious battery cell production scaling looks prudent, but delays have usurped a key competitive advantage from Ola

Given weak volume growth, cut est.

**BPCL – JEFFERIES VIEW**

- EBITDA beats estimates strongly
- Inventory gains support Q4 earnings
- Petrol/diesel marketing margins negative
- Recent fuel price hikes insufficient
- Higher crude premiums remain concern
- Freight cost pressure continues
- Rupee depreciation impacts profitability
- Longer shipping cycles raise working capital
- Net debt likely to increase
- Valuation attractive versus peers
- Trades at 1.1x forward P/B
- Strong 25% full-cycle ROE outlook

**Impact:** Attractive valuation despite near-term margin and cost pressures

**NOMURA ON BPCL**

- Maintains Buy rating on BPCL
- Target price set at ₹365
- Strong Q4 GRM aided profitability
- Inventory gains likely supported earnings
- Growth projects underway across business
- Capex intensity expected to stay high
- FY28 earnings recovery expected
- Trades at 5.9x FY28F EV/EBITDA

**Impact:** Long-term growth outlook remains positive

India Power Sector - key insights

India’s total installed capacity reached 533 GW in FY26

FY26 total capacity addition at 58 GW

Renewable generation grew 20%

Installed capacity mix
Thermal capacity at 249 GW
Solar capacity at 150 GW
Wind capacity at 56 GW
Hydro capacity at 51 GW
Nuclear capacity at 9 GW

Solar capacity has increased from 22 GW in FY18 to 150 GW in FY26

That is almost 7x growth in 8 years
FY30E at 288 GW

Cell manufacturing capacity is expected to rise from 25 GW in FY25 to 115 GW by FY30E

Ingot-wafer capacity is expected to rise from 2 GW in FY25 to 80 GW by FY30E

Polysilicon capacity is also expected to start scaling from FY27 onwards

Earlier, the opportunity was mainly in module assembly

Now the opportunity is expanding into cells, wafers, ingots & polysilicon

TVS Motors - Morgan Stanley Bullish

MS on TVS Motors – Tactical BUY
Maintain Overweight with TP of Rs 4327

This is because of a stock repurchase plan

Given the company's past record, valuations, and upcoming catalysts, expect the stock to outperform

For the first 19 days of May, two wheeler industry volumes are up 6% YoY, while TVS has seen 9.5% YoY volume growth

Expect May wholesale to grow around 16% YoY vs. 7% YoY in April, as supply has normalized

TVS trades at 16.5x FY28 EV/EBITDA, vs. Bajaj at 16x and Eicher at 19x

Think TVS should trade at a premium vs. peers, given its broad-based product portfolio, market share gains, and upside margin potential

MOTILAL OSWAL ON GE VERNOVA T&D INDIA

Maintain Buy with target price of ₹5,200 (earlier ₹4,750)

Revenue came in line with estimates, while profitability beat expectations

Domestic transmission pipeline remains healthy

Brokerage highlighted execution timeline of the recent HVDC win as a key monitorable

Export opportunities remain strong, driven by rising HVDC and data-center demand

Strong margin performance led to an upgrade in estimates

Capex plans aimed at catering to robust domestic and export demand

Estimates raised by ~7%/8% for FY27/FY28

Expects revenue/EBITDA/PAT CAGR of ~29%/31%/31% over FY26-28

NUVAMA ON ZEE ENTERTAINMENT

Maintain Buy with target price of ₹112 (earlier ₹133)

Middle East crisis impacted March quarter business performance

Brokerage cut FY27E/FY28E EPS estimates by ~18% each

Zee5 profitability improved significantly

EMKAY ON INDRAPRASTHA GAS

Maintain Add with a target price of ₹180 vs 190 (Upside ~13.9%)

Raise FY28E EPS by 3% on better volumes

Maintain Neutral with target price of ₹1,080 (earlier ₹935)

Double-digit revenue growth driven by India and EM business, partly offset by softer US/API sales

Brokerage raised FY27/FY28 earnings estimates by ~5%/4%

Near-term leverage elevated, normalization expected post ramp-

Gen Z's Sobriety Shift: A Global & Indian Reality 
 

Global alcohol giants have shed $830 billion in market value since 2021 (46% drop), driven by Gen Z drinking ~20% less than Millennials at the same age. Health awareness, wellness priorities, economic pressures, and alternatives like cannabis fuel this secular change.  
 
In India, the trend aligns with data: NFHS-5 shows declining alcohol use among men (29% to 19%), with even lower rates among youth. Strict laws, social norms, and rising fitness focus keep Gen Z consumption modest compared to Western peers. 
Industry response includes non-alcoholic innovation, but the consumer-led shift toward moderation appears structural, not cyclical. 
 
Bottom Line: This data-driven generational move signals long-term pressure on traditional alcohol markets, pushing adaptation toward healthier, premium, and experiential offerings worldwide—including India.

Believe Titan can manage the ongoing situation well by keep finding new gold sourcing avenues

MORGAN STANLEY ON BlackBuck

• Maintain Equal-weight rating; Target Price ₹650

• Adjusted EBITDA missed consensus estimates marginally

• Topline beat driven by both core and growth businesses

• Tolling business GTV grew 21% YoY

• Transacting customers increased 13.2% YoY

• Users availing two or more services rose 19.9% YoY

• Vehicle finance business nearing EBITDA breakeven

• Management remains cautious in near term due to West Asian conflict impact on freight movement
